extraction.one_line_summary: MEIJS Jordi won the Champs-Élysées sprint in a photo finish over PHILIPSEN Jasper, while VINGEGAARD Jonas secured his second consecutive Tour de France overall victory.
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR Tadej attacked in final 50km despite yellow jersey secured, forcing others to mark rather than cooperate.
ALPECIN-DECEUNINCK led out PHILIPSEN but MEIJS positioned right-hand side opposite leadout train to steal victory in photo.
JUMBO-VISMA defended yellow jersey casually allowing breakaway chaos, only TREK-SEGAFREDO chased to protect sprinter interests.

rider_signals.MEIJS Jordi: positive: Tour debut winner on Champs-Élysées reveals tactical maturity to avoid leadout trains and finish opposite side
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: neutral: attacking for stage win despite losing GC shows competitive mentality but inability to find willing partners
rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: negative: lost photo finish despite perfect ALPECIN leadout suggests positioning error or insufficient final kick variation
